questions.q: In which Indian city is the Physical Research Laboratory (PRL) located?
questions.explanation: The Physical Research Laboratory was established and is headquartered in Ahmedabad, Gujarat.

questions.q: Who founded the Physical Research Laboratory in 1947?
questions.explanation: Physicist Vikram Sarabhai founded PRL in 1947, and it became the cradle of India's space research.

questions.q: In which year was the Physical Research Laboratory founded?
questions.explanation: PRL was founded in 1947, the same year India gained independence.

questions.q: PRL is widely described as the 'Cradle' of which field in India?
questions.explanation: PRL is known as the cradle of space science in India, the birthplace of the country's space research.

questions.q: Vikram Sarabhai, the founder of PRL, is best remembered as the father of which Indian programme?
questions.explanation: Vikram Sarabhai is widely regarded as the father of the Indian space programme.

questions.q: PRL today functions as a research institution under which Indian government department?
questions.explanation: PRL operates under the Department of Space, Government of India.

questions.q: Which branch of physics did the early research at PRL primarily focus on?
options: Cosmic rays and the upper atmosphere
questions.explanation: Early PRL research concentrated on cosmic rays and properties of the upper atmosphere, Sarabhai's specialty.

questions.q: Which institution did Vikram Sarabhai's family help found in the same city, supporting management education there?
questions.explanation: Vikram Sarabhai was a key force behind the establishment of the Indian Institute of Management, Ahmedabad.

questions.q: PRL is regarded as a forerunner institution to which Indian space agency?
questions.explanation: Work begun at PRL led directly to the creation of ISRO, the Indian Space Research Organisation.

questions.q: In which Indian state is PRL situated?
questions.explanation: PRL is located in Ahmedabad, which is in the state of Gujarat.

questions.q: Two organizations jointly provided the initial financial support to establish PRL in 1947. One was the Karmkshetra Educational Foundation; what was the other?
questions.explanation: PRL was initially supported by the Karmkshetra Educational Foundation and the Ahmedabad Education Society.

questions.q: At which institution did Vikram Sarabhai conduct his early cosmic-ray research before returning to India to found PRL?
questions.explanation: Sarabhai did early cosmic-ray work at the Indian Institute of Science in Bangalore under C. V. Raman, and later earned his doctorate from Cambridge.

questions.q: PRL operates a major astronomical observatory at Mount Abu. In which state is Mount Abu?
questions.explanation: PRL runs the Mount Abu Infrared Observatory in Rajasthan, used for infrared and optical astronomy.

questions.q: Vikram Sarabhai earned his PhD from the University of Cambridge on a thesis concerning which subject?
questions.explanation: Sarabhai's Cambridge doctoral work was on cosmic ray investigations, the field that shaped PRL's early research.

questions.q: After Vikram Sarabhai's death in 1971, which eminent geophysicist took over as director of PRL, serving from 1972 to 1983?
questions.explanation: Devendra Lal became director of PRL in 1972, succeeding Vikram Sarabhai, and led the institute until 1983. (K. R. Ramanathan was PRL's first director, before Sarabhai.)

questions.q: PRL scientists contributed to analyzing lunar samples. From which historic mission did such samples first become widely available to researchers worldwide?
questions.explanation: NASA's Apollo missions returned lunar samples, and PRL was among institutions that studied them.

questions.q: Which of these research areas is a recognized focus at PRL today?
questions.explanation: Modern PRL conducts research in astronomy, astrophysics, planetary sciences, atmospheric science, geosciences and theoretical physics.

questions.q: PRL was instrumental in setting up India's first rocket-launching station in 1963 at which coastal location?
questions.explanation: Sarabhai and PRL helped establish the Thumba Equatorial Rocket Launching Station (TERLS) near Thiruvananthapuram in 1963.

questions.q: Which subject area, central to atmospheric and space physics, was a hallmark of PRL's research on the ionized upper atmosphere?
questions.explanation: PRL was a leading center for ionospheric and upper-atmosphere physics, building on Sarabhai's cosmic-ray interests.

questions.q: Vikram Sarabhai, PRL's founder, was awarded which of India's high civilian honours?
options: Padma Bhushan and the posthumous Padma Vibhushan
questions.explanation: Sarabhai received the Padma Bhushan in 1966 and was awarded the Padma Vibhushan posthumously in 1972.

questions.q: The PRL-operated observatory at Mount Abu is particularly noted for observations in which part of the spectrum?
questions.explanation: The Mount Abu Infrared Observatory specializes in infrared astronomy, along with optical work.

questions.q: PRL's PARAS spectrograph, mounted on the Mount Abu telescope, is designed primarily to detect what?
questions.explanation: The PARAS (PRL Advanced Radial-velocity Abu-sky Search) spectrograph is used to discover and characterize exoplanets.

questions.q: Using the PARAS instrument, PRL scientists announced the discovery of an exoplanet around which star, a notable Indian exoplanet detection?
questions.explanation: In 2018 PRL announced the discovery of the sub-Saturn exoplanet K2-236b (around EPIC 211945201) using the PARAS spectrograph.

questions.q: Devendra Lal, a celebrated PRL scientist and later director, was internationally renowned for pioneering work in which area?
questions.explanation: Devendra Lal was a world authority on cosmic-ray-produced (cosmogenic) nuclides and their use in Earth and ocean sciences.

questions.q: K. R. Ramanathan, PRL's first director, made early-career contributions to the study of which atmospheric phenomenon, related to C. V. Raman's work?
options: Molecular scattering of light in the atmosphere
questions.explanation: Ramanathan worked on molecular scattering of light and atmospheric ozone, linking to Raman's scattering research.

questions.q: PRL maintains a noble-gas and isotope laboratory used in meteorite studies. Analysis of meteorites at PRL primarily helps reconstruct what?
options: The early history and age of the Solar System
questions.explanation: PRL's noble-gas and isotope work on meteorites helps determine ages and processes from the early Solar System.

questions.q: Besides Mount Abu, PRL operates a high-altitude research facility for atmospheric and astronomical studies at Udaipur, known by what name?
questions.explanation: The Udaipur Solar Observatory, associated with PRL's solar physics research, studies the Sun from a lake-island site.

questions.q: Vikram Sarabhai, who founded PRL, also concurrently chaired which national commission at the time of his death in 1971?
questions.explanation: Sarabhai succeeded Homi Bhabha as Chairman of the Atomic Energy Commission of India, a post he held until his death in 1971.

questions.q: PRL contributed scientific payloads and analysis to India's first lunar mission. Which mission was this?
questions.explanation: PRL contributed to Chandrayaan-1, India's first lunar mission, and continues to support planetary exploration efforts.

questions.q: The Mars Orbiter Mission carried an instrument with PRL involvement to study the Martian atmosphere. Which Martian gas, of astrobiological interest, was a key target of such atmospheric studies?
questions.explanation: The Mars Orbiter Mission included a methane sensor; methane is of strong astrobiological interest, and PRL contributed to Martian atmospheric science.
